failed_attempts: n)

    | succ n ih' =>

have h₂ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, sin x * f (cos x) = ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, sin x * f (cos x) := rfl
    -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
    have h₃ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, sin x * f (cos x) = ∫ y in (-1 : ℝ)..(1 : ℝ), f y := by
      have h₄ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, sin x * f (cos x) = ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, f (cos x) * sin x := by
        congr
        ext x
        ring
      rw [h₄]
      -- Use the substitution u = cos x
      have h₅ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, f (cos x) * sin x = ∫ y in (-1 : ℝ)..(1 : ℝ), f y := by
        have h₆ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, f (cos x) * sin x = -∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, f (cos x) * (-sin x) := by
          simp [intervalIntegral.integral_neg]
          <;>
          congr 1 <;>
          ext x <;>
          ring
        rw [h₆]
        have h₇ : -∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, f (cos x) * (-sin x) = ∫ y in (-1 : ℝ)..(1 : ℝ), f y := by
          -- Use the substitution u = cos x
          have h₈ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, f (cos x) * (-sin x) = ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, (-sin x) * f (cos x) := by
            congr
            ext x
            ring
          rw [h₈]
          -- Use the substitution u = cos x
          have h₉ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, (-sin x) * f (cos x) = ∫ y in (cos π)..(cos 0), f y := by
            have h₁₀ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, (-sin x) * f (cos x) = ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, (deriv (fun x : ℝ => cos x) x) * f (cos x) := by
              congr
              ext x
              have h₁₁ : deriv (fun x : ℝ => cos x) x = -sin x := by
                rw [show deriv (fun x : ℝ => cos x) x = -sin x by
                  -- Differentiate cos x
                  simp [deriv_cos]
                  <;>
                  ring_nf]
              rw [h₁₁]
              <;>
              ring
            rw [h₁₀]
            -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
            have h₁₂ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, (deriv (fun x : ℝ => cos x) x) * f (cos x) = ∫ y in (cos π)..(cos 0), f y := by
              have h₁₃ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, (deriv (fun x : ℝ => cos x) x) * f (cos x) = ∫ y in (cos π)..(cos 0), f y := by
                -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
                have h₁₄ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, (deriv (fun x : ℝ => cos x) x) * f (cos x) = ∫ y in (cos π)..(cos 0), f y := by
                  -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
                  rw [intervalIntegral.integral_comp_deriv_sub_left (fun x => cos x) (by continuity) (by
                    -- Check that cos x is differentiable on [0, π]
                    intro x hx
                    -- cos x is differentiable everywhere
                    continuity)]
                  <;>
                  simp [cos_zero, cos_pi]
                  <;>
                  norm_num
                  <;>
                  ring_nf
                  <;>
                  simp_all [cos_zero, cos_pi]
                  <;>
                  norm_num
                  <;>
                  linarith
                exact h₁₄
              exact h₁₃
            rw [h₁₂]
          rw [h₉]
          -- Simplify the limits of integration
          have h₁₀ : (cos π : ℝ) = -1 := by norm_num
          have h₁₁ : (cos 0 : ℝ) = 1 := by norm_num
          rw [h₁₀, h₁₁]
          <;>
          simp [intervalIntegral.integral_symm]
          <;>
          ring_nf
          <;>
          simp_all [cos_zero, cos_pi]
          <;>
          norm_num
          <;>
          linarith
        rw [h₇]
        <;>
        simp [intervalIntegral.integral_symm]
        <;>
        ring_nf
        <;>
        simp_all [cos_zero, cos_pi]
        <;>
        norm_num
        <;>
        linarith
      rw [h₅]
    rw [h₃]
  rw [h₁]
  <;>
  simp [h₀]
  <;>
  ring_nf
  <;>
  simp_all [cos_zero, cos_pi]
  <;>
  norm_num
  <;>
  linarith

Nat.rec (fun n => n + 1)
      (fun m am n =>

funext m n
  induction m with
  | zero => simp [A, myA]
  | succ m ih =>
    induction n with
    | zero =>
      simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
      <;> try simp_all (config := {decide := true})

      <;> try ring_nf at *
      <;> try simp_all (config := {decide := true})
      <;> try simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
      <;> try decide
      <;> try simp_all (config := {decide := true})
    | succ n ih' =>
      simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
      <;> try simp_all (config := {decide := true})
      <;> try simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
      <;> try decide
      <;> try simp_all (config := {decide := true})

funext m n
  have h : ∀ m n : ℕ, A m n = myA m n := by
    intro m n
    induction m generalizing n with
    | zero =>
      simp [A, myA]
      <;>
      induction n <;> simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
      <;>
      ring_nf at * <;>
      omega
    | succ m ih =>
      induction n with
      | zero =>
        simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
        <;>
        try omega
        <;>
        try ring_nf at * <;>
        try simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
        <;>
        try omega
      | succ n ih' =>
        simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
        <;>
        try omega
        <;>
        try ring_nf at * <;>
        try simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
        <;>
        try omega
        <;>
        try linarith
        <;>
        try
          {
            have h₁ := ih n
            have h₂ := ih'
            simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
            <;>
            try omega
            <;>
            try ring_nf at * <;>
            try simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
            <;>
            try omega
          }
  have h₁ := h m n
  simp_all

funext m n
  have h : A m n = myA m n := by
    induction m with
    | zero =>
      -- Base case: when m = 0, A 0 n = n + 1 and myA 0 n = n + 1
      simp [A, myA]
    | succ m ih =>
      induction n with
      | zero =>
        -- Base case: when n = 0, A (m + 1) 0 = A m 1 and myA (m + 1) 0 = A m 1
        simp_all [A, myA]
      | succ n ih' =>
        -- Inductive step: when n = k + 1, A (m + 1) (n + 1) = A m (A (m + 1) n)
        simp_all [A, myA, Nat.recOn, Nat.succ_eq_add_one]
        <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [A, myA, Nat.recOn, Nat.succ_eq_add_one]
            <;>
            omega
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [A, myA, Nat.recOn, Nat.succ_eq_add_one]
            <;>
            ring_nf at *
            <;>
            omega
          })
  exact h

funext m n
  have h : A m n = myA m n := by
    induction m with
    | zero =>
      simp [A, myA]
    | succ m ih =>
      cases n with
      | zero =>
        simp_all [A, myA]
        <;>
        (try decide) <;>
        (try simp_all [Nat.rec]) <;>
        (try ring_nf at *) <;>
        (try norm_num) <;>
        (try omega)
      | succ n =>

        <;>
        (try decide) <;>
        (try simp_all [Nat.rec]) <;>
        (try ring_nf at *) <;>
        (try norm_num) <;>
        (try omega)
        <;>
        (try {
          have h₁ := ih (n + 1)
          have h₂ := ih 0
          simp at h₁ h₂ ⊢
          <;>
          (try omega) <;>
          (try ring_nf at *) <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try simp_all [Nat.rec])
        })
        <;>
        (try {
          have h₁ := ih 0
          have h₂ := ih 1
          simp at h₁ h₂ ⊢
          <;>
          (try omega) <;>
          (try ring_nf at *) <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try simp_all [Nat.rec])
        })
        <;>
        (try {
          have h₁ := ih 1
          have h₂ := ih 2
          simp at h₁ h₂ ⊢
          <;>
          (try omega) <;>
          (try ring_nf at *) <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try simp_all [Nat.rec])
        })
  exact h

have h₂ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x + 1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = (∫ x in a..b, (1 / x : ℝ)) + ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) := by
      apply intervalIntegral.integral_add
      · apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable
        continuity
      · apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable
        continuity
    rw [h₂]
    have h₃ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x : ℝ) = log b - log a := by
      have h₄ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x : ℝ) = log b - log a := by
        -- Use the fundamental theorem of calculus to evaluate the integral of 1/x
        have h₅ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x : ℝ) = log b - log a := by
          rw [← Real.log_div (by linarith) (by linarith)]
          <;>
          simp [intervalIntegral.integral_comp_mul_left (fun x => 1 / x)]
          <;>
          field_simp [Real.log_div, Real.log_mul, Real.log_rpow, Real.log_pow]
          <;>
          ring_nf
          <;>
          norm_num
          <;>
          linarith
        exact h₅
      exact h₄
    have h₄ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = 1 / a - 1 / b := by
      -- Use the fundamental theorem of calculus to evaluate the integral of 1/x^2
      have h₅ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = 1 / a - 1 / b := by
        have h₆ : ∫ x in a..b, (1 / x ^ 2 : ℝ) = ∫ x in a..b, (x : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ 2 := by
          congr
          <;>
          funext x <;>
          field_simp <;>
          ring_nf
        rw [h₆]
        -- Use the antiderivative of x⁻¹²
        have h₇ : ∫ x in a..b, (x : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ 2 = ( - (x : ℝ)⁻¹ ) | a..b := by
          -- Use the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
          have h₈ : ∫ x in a..b, (x : ℝ)⁻¹ ^ 2 = ( - (x : ℝ)⁻¹ ) | a..b := by
            -- Compute the antiderivative and apply the limits
            norm_num [intervalIntegral.integral_comp_mul_left, mul_comm]
            <;>
            simp_all [div_pow]
            <;>
            norm_num
            <;>
            ring_nf
            <;>
            field_simp
            <;>
            norm_num
            <;>
            linarith
          exact h₈
        rw [h₇]
        -- Evaluate the antiderivative at the bounds
        have h₉ : ( - (x : ℝ)⁻¹ ) | a..b = (-(b : ℝ)⁻¹) - (-(a : ℝ)⁻¹) := by
          simp [intervalIntegral.integral_comp_mul_left]
          <;>
          norm_num
          <;>
          ring_nf
          <;>
          field_simp
          <;>
          norm_num
          <;>
          linarith
        rw [h₉]
        -- Simplify the expression
        have h₁₀ : (-(b : ℝ)⁻¹) - (-(a : ℝ)⁻¹) = 1 / a - 1 / b := by
          ring_nf
          <;>
          field_simp
          <;>
          ring_nf
          <;>
          norm_num
          <;>
          linarith
        rw [h₁₀]
      exact h₅
    rw [h₃, h₄]
    -- Combine the results
    <;>
    ring_nf
    <;>
    field_simp
    <;>
    ring_nf
    <;>
    norm_num
    <;>
    linarith
  rw [h₁]
  <;>
  ring_nf
  <;>
  field_simp
  <;>
  ring_nf
  <;>
  norm_num
  <;>
  linarith

  funext n m

      | zero =>
        simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
        <;> simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
        <;> try apply Eq.symm <;> try apply Eq.refl
      | succ n ih' =>
        simp_all [A, myA, Nat.rec]
        <;> try apply Eq.symm <;> try apply Eq.refl
  exact h m n
rfl
